entries.opportunity: Remain in current leadership track vs pivot toward principal IC path
Expand Ghostweave into productized tooling vs remain consulting-only
entries.outcome: Platform direction emerging. Ghostweave Intel (client CRM) and CreatorOS (co-founder) both active.

entries.opportunity: CreatorOS — build as Ghostweave project vs co-found with Noelle
entries.filter_reasoning: Strong technical fit and interesting architecture problem. Load is real concern. Partnership structure (equity, IP, roles) not yet formalized — this is the primary condition.
entries.outcome: Active build in progress. Noelle brings industry knowledge, creator network, investor contacts. Core architecture scaffolded on GitHub. Pilot creator (Thrace) identified. Partnership structure still open.

entries.opportunity: Apply for Senior Director, Data Workforce Insights & Systems at Northwestern Mutual
entries.filter_reasoning: Strong technical match. Recruiter-initiated is a positive signal. In-office requirement low (1-2x/month). Compensation band wide — must anchor upper half. Primary condition: HR ops gap needs framing mitigation.
entries.outcome: Applied. Resume tailored with strengthened people leadership numbers and governance language. Formal interviews not yet scheduled.

entries.opportunity: Expel Analytics Engineering / Platform Lead — continue active pursuit
entries.filter_reasoning: Strong stack alignment (dbt, BigQuery). Principal IC + small team lead structure fits direction. Clear operational problems to solve (lineage, metric trust). Stakeholder Steven Bowen identified as key relationship to establish.
entries.outcome: Active. Stakeholder interview with Steven Bowen (FP&A Manager) in preparation. 90-day win sequence framed.

entries.opportunity: Participate in Atlassian ShipIt 62 hackathon with TeamFlow pitch
entries.filter_reasoning: Genuine technical fit with people core data model work. Differentiating angle given background. Load is real — hackathon window (April 16-20) coincides with Milwaukee Half (April 18). Condition: scope the build tightly so it's executable in the window.
entries.outcome: In planning. Gap analysis complete. Day-by-day execution plan scoped.

entries.opportunity: Expel Analytics Engineering / Platform Lead — accept vs decline offer
entries.source: active interview process — reached offer stage
entries.non_negotiable_violated: Compensation must maintain Coast FIRE trajectory
entries.filter_reasoning: Offer would be ~$195k base, max $210k negotiated. That's a $79-82k/year cut vs. Atlassian total comp of $375k. As primary income earner with wife not working, this isn't abstract — it directly impacts household security. Work sounds interesting and people are good, but it's not a dream company. Validation high from being chosen nearly overrode the financial reality. Therapist framing: 'Why switch for a pay cut for something that isn't a dream company? Just keep getting paid until they make you go away.' The correct insight: the interest in Expel was 80% validation-seeking, 20% actual fit.
entries.outcome: Declined. Left door open gracefully. Core learning: identified the validation loop pattern — chasing the high of being chosen rather than evaluating actual fit. This pattern has been operating across multiple job searches.

entries.opportunity: Northwestern Mutual Senior Director, Data Workforce Insights & Systems — continue vs withdraw
entries.source: recruiter outreach — early stage, interviews not yet scheduled
entries.filter_reasoning: After Expel clarity session, recognized that Senior Director roles at large financial institutions replicate the exact burnout pattern: politics, roadmapping, governance, sustaining work, visibility games. The title and comp are attractive but the actual work content is misaligned with what actually energizes. More of the same that has burned out at every prior employer.
entries.outcome: Withdrew from process. Not pursuing further director-track roles at large orgs. Reorienting: stay at Atlassian, test Ghostweave Labs seriously for 3-4 years, reassess at September 2026 checkpoint.
